#### Current Behavior
- Type checking in `*stack` operations (e.g., `numpy.stack`, `numpy.vstack`, `numpy.hstack`) is currently performed by checking if the input has the `__getitem__` attribute.
- This approach leads to unexpected behavior when the input is an array:
  - Example: `array([2,5,7])` is split into multiple single-element arrays: `(array([2]), array([5]), array([7]))`.

#### Proposed Change
- Enforce the input to be a sequence, as specified in the NumPy documentation: [numpy.stack](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.stack.html).

This prevents unintended splitting of array inputs, which can lead to confusion and poor performance in user code.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27317/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27317/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2326946203","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27317#issuecomment-2326946203","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27317","id":2326946203,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86KsmGb","user":{"login":"Kh4L","id":3193578,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMxOTM1Nzg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/3193578?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Kh4L","html_url":"https://github.com/Kh4L","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Kh4L/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Kh4L/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Kh4L/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Kh4L/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Kh4L/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Kh4L/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Kh4L/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Kh4L/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Kh4L/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-03T16:24:24Z","updated_at":"2024-09-03T16:24:24Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"@jorenham thank you for the detailed review.

@jorenham here is a small repro:

```
import numpy as np
import timeit

large_array = np.random.rand(2**18)

def stack_direct():
    np.stack(large_array)

def stack_tuple():
    np.stack((large_array,))

time_direct = timeit.timeit(stack_direct, number=3)
time_tuple = timeit.timeit(stack_tuple, number=3)

print(f"Time for np.stack with array directly: {time_direct:.6f} seconds")
print(f"Time for np.stack with array as a tuple: {time_tuple:.6f} seconds")
```

output:
```
$ python repro.py
Time for np.stack with array directly: 0.454974 seconds
Time for np.stack with array as a tuple: 0.000284 seconds
```

In `stack_direct`, the array is splitted into `2**18` single-element arrays in https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/9a2c063342284177e9f51a29f3e616309a3400b1/numpy/_core/shape_base.py#L217

output after the proposed change:

```
$ python repro.py
Time for np.stack with array directly: 0.000282 seconds
Time for np.stack with array as a tuple: 0.000284 seconds
```

#### Current Behavior
- Type checking in `*stack` operations (e.g., `numpy.stack`, `numpy.vstack`, `numpy.hstack`) is currently performed by checking if the input has the `__getitem__` attribute.
- This approach leads to unexpected behavior when the input is an array:
  - Example: `array([2,5,7])` is split into multiple single-element arrays: `(array([2]), array([5]), array([7]))`.

#### Proposed Change
- Treat single array as a sequence of size 1 (a single array), to be compliant with the NumPy documentation: [numpy.stack](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.stack.html).

#### Current Behavior
- Type checking in `*stack` operations (e.g., `numpy.stack`, `numpy.vstack`, `numpy.hstack`) is currently performed by checking if the input has the `__getitem__` attribute.
- This approach leads to unexpected behavior when the input is an array:
  - Example: `array([2,5,7])` is split into multiple single-element arrays: `(array([2]), array([5]), array([7]))`.

#### Proposed Change
- Enforce the input to be a sequence, as specified in the NumPy documentation: [numpy.stack](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.stack.html).

This prevents unintended splitting of array inputs, which can lead to confusion and poor performance in user
